channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Fact-check fight: CBS News cuts candidates' mics after JD Vance objects to a moderator's correction
item.description: CBS News briefly cut off the microphones of vice presidential candidates JD Vance and Tim Walz during an exchange in their debate Tuesday when the Republican objected to an attempt by moderator Margaret Brennan to correct something he said about immigration.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden estimates recovery could cost billions ahead of visit to Helene-raved Carolinas
item.description: President Joe Biden will survey the devastation in North and South Carolina as rescuers continued their search for anyone still unaccounted for since Hurricane Helene’s deadly remnants caused catastrophic damage across the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: Erik Verduzco, Jeff Amy And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Clues that could have helped solve the Texas Killing Fields murders in an all-new episode of ‘The Evidence Room’
item.description: A new episode of the KPRC 2 Investigates true crime docuseries 'The Evidence Room' premieres Wednesday at 6:30 p.m. exclusively on the KPRC 2+ livestream.
item.dc:creator: Robert Arnold, Tiffani Lupenski, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris and Biden are fanning out across the Southeast as devastation from Helene grows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Over the last four years, President Joe Biden has jetted off to survey damage and console victims after tornadoes, wildfires and tropical storms. It's not a role that Kamala Harris has played as vice president.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: China marks 75 years of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China is marking the 75th year of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger over the massive state.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US 'Welcome Corps' helps resettle LGBTQ+ refugees fleeing crackdowns against gay people
item.description: A program that helps refugees resettle in the United States has expanded to include LGBTQ+ people who have been threatened and harassed in their home countries.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey And Terry Chea, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KREX 5

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: "So first of all, injured. What does injured mean? Injured means — you mean because they had a headache? Because the bombs never hit the fort," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal prosecutors file new indictment against ex-Louisville police officers
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Federal prosecutors filed a new indictment Tuesday against two former Louisville officers accused of falsifying a warrant that led police to Breonna Taylor’s door before they fatally shot her.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: School board to pay $575K to teacher fired for not using transgender student's pronouns
item.description: A Virginia school board has agreed to pay a former high school teacher more than $500,000 in damages and attorneys' fees as part of a legal settlement after the instructor's dismissal in 2018 for refusing to use a transgender student's pronouns.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9

item.title: School board to pay $575K to teacher fired for not using transgender student's pronouns
item.description: “Peter wasn’t fired for something he said; he was fired for something he couldn’t say,” ADF senior counsel Tyson Langhofer said.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: California firefighters in Hurricane Helene relief team recovering after crash in Texas
item.description: In a statement on Facebook, the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department thanked the community for their support during this difficult time.
item.dc:creator: Christa Swanson, NewsNation

item.title: Cook Political Report shifts Texas Senate race toward Democrats
item.description: The Cook Political Report on Tuesday shifted Texas’s Senate race from "likely Republican" to "lean Republican," signaling momentum for Rep. Colin Allred's (D) challenge against incumbent Sen. Ted Cruz (R) in the red state.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Dockworkers may have the negotiating advantage in their strike against US ports
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The 45,000 dockworkers who went on strike Tuesday for the first time in decades at 36 U.S. ports from Maine to Texas may wield the upper hand in their standoff with port operators over wages and the use of automation.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and TASSANEE VEJPONGSA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Impact Plastics addresses 'missing and deceased employees' after floods
item.description: "We are devastated by the tragic loss of great employees,” Impact Plastics founder Gerald O'Connor said in the release.
item.dc:creator: Murry Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Ken Page, who starred in 'Cats' on Broadway and voiced Oogie Boogie, dies at 70
item.description: Ken Page, a stage and screen actor who starred alongside Beyoncé in “Dreamgirls,” introduced Broadway audiences to Old Deuteronomy in “Cats” and scared generations of kids as the voice of Oogie Boogie, the villain of the 1993 animated holiday film ”The Nightmare Before Christmas,″ has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hair found in truck helps police identify suspect in 1995 disappearance of Morgan Nick, 6
item.description: ALMA, Ark. (AP) — Hair found in a truck once owned by an Arkansas man who died in prison more than two decades ago was the clue investigators needed to connect the dead inmate to the disappearance of a 6-year-old girl who went missing in 1995.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DEMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tennessee migrant workers arrested for allegedly looting Hurricane Helene devastation
item.description: Washington County, Tennessee, authorities arrested eight migrants accused of looting residential homes badly damaged by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Publix donates $1 million to support disaster relief following Hurricane Helene
item.description: Florida-based grocery chain, Publix, announced it's making a major contribution to help relief efforts in areas affected by the catastrophic damage left behind by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Fox 5

item.title: Israel says Iran has fired missiles at Israel and it warned residents to shelter in place
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Israeli military said Tuesday that Iran has fired missiles at Israel, and air raid sirens sounded across the country as residents were ordered to remain close to bomb shelters. Israel and the United States have warned there would be severe consequences if Iran attacks.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Eric Adams calls out alleged collusion of feds and mainstream media, asks judge for 'consequences'
item.description: Lawyers for New York City Mayor Eric Adams are seeking sanctions against the Department of Justice, which they accuse of leaking details of his indictment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Murders, mayhem and officer's gunfire lead to charges at Brooklyn jail where 'Diddy' is held
item.description: Two inmates stabbed to death. Another speared in the spine with a makeshift icepick. A correctional officer charged with shooting at a car during an unauthorized high-speed chase.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Kirk becomes a hurricane as North Carolina reels from Helene
item.description: Desperate residents of the storm-battered mountains of western North Carolina lined up for water and food, hunted for cellphone signals and slogged buckets from creeks to flush toilets days after Hurricane Helene’s remnants deluged the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Helene and other storms dumped a whopping 40 trillion gallons of rain on the South
item.description: More than 40 trillion gallons of rain drenched the Southeast United States in the last week from Hurricane Helene and a run-of-the-mill rainstorm that sloshed in ahead of it — an unheard of amount of water that has stunned experts.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN AP Science Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump and Harris' running mates are set to face off and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Hampshire parents sue school district for banning them over 'silent protest' against trans soccer player
item.description: A group of New Hampshire parents filed a lawsuit against a school district over their removal from a girls soccer game for protesting against a transgender player.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge dismisses nursing home COVID death case against former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo
item.description: A federal judge on Monday dismissed a lawsuit against former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo over his handling of nursing home deaths during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump launches GoFundMe to help Hurricane Helene victims, raises more than $1M
item.description: A GoFundMe launched by the Trump campaign raised more than $1 million on Monday after he visited areas of Georgia hit by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Toy gun-wielding teenager shot after allegedly attempting to rob armed Pennsylvania man
item.description: A 16-year-old was shot in Philadelphia after allegedly trying to rob a man of his wallet, keys and other belongings while wielding a toy gun on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-University of Kentucky staffer asks if Hurricane Helene is act of God to punish MAGA supporters
item.description: A former staffer at the University of Kentucky floated the idea that God was using Hurricane Helene to punish Trump supporters in a social media post.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FBI to pay $22M to settle claims of sexual discrimination at training academy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI agreed Monday to pay more than $22 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging female recruits were singled out for dismissal in training and routinely harassed by instructors with sexually charged comments about their breast size, false allegations of infidelity and
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams accepted harmless 'courtesies,' not bribes, his lawyer says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams launched a legal attack on the federal corruption case against him Monday, with his attorney asking a judge to toss out bribery charges and then holding a combative news conference accusing prosecutors of ethical lapses.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Spooky fall adventures in Europe's most haunted destinations
item.description: Europe is a continent steeped in history, and with that history comes tales of restless spirits, haunted castles and ghostly encounters.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Submerged EVs can catch fire when inundated with saltwater: What to know
item.description: Electric vehicles can catch fire if they are inundated by saltwater, so owners who live in the path of a major storm should take precautions and prepare for the possibility that they'll be unable to charge their cars during a power outage.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5

item.title: Exploring the Eric Adams indictment for bribery, wire fraud, and solicitating contributions from Turkish nationals
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams pleaded not guilty in a federal bribery case after being indicted on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, WFLA, NBC
